meaning: adj.不受拘束的,不受强制的,不做作的
examples: Banks will go from being unconstrained by their balance-sheets to being caged by them.
If tax cutting is not a path to automatic prosperity, neither is unconstrained social spending.
You passed something known to be a t, an unconstrained type parameter, and hence it could be anything.
There are very few people in the world who actually use unconstrained critical thinking.
He switched tracks when he saw an opportunity in the huge, unconstrained mines available there.
